prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "单腿臀桥保持" (single-leg glute bridge hold) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person holding a single-leg glute bridge in side profile: lying SUPINE on the floor with the entire upper back, both shoulder blades, the head, and the neck all flat on the ground; both arms resting flat on the floor at the sides, palms down. The RIGHT foot is flat on the ground with the right knee bent at exactly 90 degrees — this is the supporting leg. The LEFT LEG is the lifted leg and MUST be FULLY EXTENDED STRAIGHT FORWARD AND PERFECTLY HORIZONTAL — parallel to the floor — at the same height as the right thigh. The left leg is NOT angled upward, NOT bent at the knee, NOT pointing toward the ceiling. The left foot, the hips, and the right shoulder must all line up on one straight HORIZONTAL line parallel to the ground. Hips are lifted high enough so that there is a clear straight line from the supporting knee through the hips to the shoulders. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL: the lifted leg is HORIZONTAL pointing forward, NOT diagonally upward.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: gluteus maximus and hamstring group of the supporting (right) leg. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: core muscles and erector spinae and gluteus medius for hip stability. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "臀大肌" pointing to the gluteus maximus of the supporting leg; "腘绳肌" pointing to the hamstring group of the supporting leg; "核心 / 竖脊肌" pointing to the core and erector spinae area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
